What's up everybody? This is Russell Brunson and I want to welcome you guys to the 10X Secrets Masterclass. I'm so excited for today. And I hope you guys are as excited as well. So kind of some of the background for those, I think most of you guys know what's happening here, but we had a really fun, unique chance about a month and a half, maybe two months ago now, to speak at Grant Cardone's 10X event. I knew it was a big opportunity. I spoke at his event last year and at the event we did about a million dollars in sales. And I was like, I want to do something that I've never done before. And I was like, I want to net a million dollars in sales from this thing. And he was, I told me there's gonna be 9,000 people in this audience and this auditorium and like, said that was the goal though. Okay, how do we do net a million dollars? I was like, it's net a million dollars. I gotta make about three because he gets his cut and all those other things. We did the whole math behind it. So we tried to figure it out. And we spent the next two or three months choreographing the entire process to be able to do it. And it obviously worked really, really well. And we broke the record, which was three million dollars. I don't know what the record was, that's what we got. And it worked. It was amazing. And then what's cool, and I'll tell you more of the story about this later, is that after the first night of processing all the orders, we got a total and we're all excited. We called Grant and Jared and they were freaking out. And I said, hey, I think we can close more sales. You'll be back on stage tomorrow for like five minutes. And so they did and we ended up pushing about another $250,000 in sales. And the reason why is I said, how many of you guys here would like to see what I just did? And what's kind of funny is there's 9,000 salespeople at this event. So they're all salespeople and everyone kept telling me, man, I thought I was the best salesperson in the world, but you destroyed it. Like you are the greatest of all time. And I'm like, well, thank you. And many laughed, but I was like, the difference is that I'm not actually a very good salesperson. If you see me come up to somebody one-on-one, I am by far the worst ever. In fact, I have sales guys and they used to, every once in a while, like someone, they were like, hey, if you can get on the phone with this person and just kind of push them over the edge, I'm like, oh, sure, I get on the phone with them and I always talk the person out of the cell. Like I'm actually a really, really bad salesperson, but I'm really, really good at selling to the masses. And so it's different. It's a different type of selling that I believe works in any business, any industry. And so I came back on stage with five minutes and said, how many of you guys would like to learn how to sell the way that I did? And everyone went crazy. And I said, okay, what we're going to do is for any of you guys who already got signed up yesterday, you'll get this class for free. For anyone who gets signed today, though, I'm going to do a class. I'm going to show you guys behind the scenes what I did and why I did and how the whole thing works. And that was kind of game plan. And like I said, we got another $250,000 in sales came from that. And so that's what this is for. This is for all of you guys here. I've also invited some of my top coaching clients and to listen as well. And so that's kind of the game plan. And a lot of you guys have asked me how long is this training going to be? And the answer is as long as it needs to be. So I've got the rest of the day blocked out for this. It might be two hours, it might be five, it might be six. I don't know. I'm going to go until I stop having fun and it's going to be really, really good. So basically I'm going to break this down into three different sessions. It's kind of the game plan. So session number one, I'm going to be talking about creating the right offer. Okay. When you're selling one-on-one, it's different, right? Like I'm selling one-on-one. I talk to the person, I fill them out. I can create an offer based on the fly and what they actually need, right? When I'm selling to the masses, it's different. I have to create an offer in a special way to be able to try to close as many people at once. That's going to be the first session. And then we'll take a little bit of a break and we'll come back in the second session I'm going to be covering is what we call the perfect webinar. If you've read my expert secrets book or the dot com secrets book, I talk a lot about this, but there's been a lot of things we've tweaked and changed and a lot of things I did specifically for this event. I'm going to walk you guys through what the whole perfect webinar is, how the stories work together because again, it's the key to mass selling. And then the third session when we come back, we're actually going to watch the entire presentation. So I'm going to push play. We're going to watch the presentation. I'm going to pause and be like, that's why I did that thing. And remember I talked about this before. This is why I did that. And we're going to go through the whole thing together and that way you'll see the presentation. But also, but more importantly, be able to see like we'll be stopping to talk about what I did, why I did it, and kind of what's happening. So that's the game plan for today. It should be a lot of fun. That presentation was 90 minutes. So that alone should probably take about three hours for me to actually go through the whole thing. Just FYI. Yeah, probably. So that'll be the longest session. Like so probably breaks in between and stuff like that. That's kind of the game plan. And so that said, I'm excited. I've been looking forward to talking about this for a month and a half now and actually sharing all these things. Now, depending on where you are in my ecosystem, again, some of you guys, first time you ever heard about me was at the 10X events. Some of you guys have read my books. You've been on my podcast. And you guys have been more advanced. Some of these things are going to be reviewed because some stuff, stuff that I've talked about other places, some stuff to be brand new. There's things that I've tested with I never publicly talked about. Like for example, at 10X event, I did something that I learned from one of my friends, Myron Golden, who's one of the best closers on planet Earth, I think all the price marinated. And I'd never, I did it before in a webinar kind of, but I'd never done it before. I was so scared on 10X stage. I'll show you that part. I was like trembling because I'd never done it before and I was doing it for 9,000 people. I was so scared to do it. But it was one of the biggest components to actually price that and get people to know what the value was later on. And I'm going to show you guys that whole thing. Like I've never talked about it anywhere. It's not in the books, not in the podcast. It's like, it's just, this is the first time I've talked about that. A lot of other things like that as well. A lot of things like, why did I get everybody to pull their phone out and have their camera lit up? You know, number one, I wanted a really cool picture. I'm not going to lie, but there was a strategy and a reason behind that. There's all these little things that we do that I've never talked about before and I've never had really a format to do that. So all those things would be here. So as you're going through this, again, some of you guys are like, oh, I've heard us talk about that. I understand that. And hopefully those, the repetition of it will get you guys to understand it deeper and actually be able to execute on it. And the second side is new stuff that you can be like, oh my gosh, I never knew that that's what he was doing and why he was doing it and hope you guys to add those little tweaks and changes into whatever kind of presentation you're doing. So that's kind of game plan. I've got my notes here. So if I'm referring over here, it's because I'm going through my notes to make sure I don't miss anything. All right. So again, the whole core concept of this presentation is mass selling. How do we sell to the masses? Okay. Now, again, I know a lot of guys who are on here are really good salespeople, but selling to masses is different than selling one-on-one. Okay. Because one-on-one, again, if I'm talking to you, I can figure out what your concerns are. I can resolve those concerns. I can customize the package. I can do all sorts of stuff that's really powerful one-on-one. So I love, well, I don't personally love one-on-one sales. I love having one-on-one sales happening in my, or in my companies because we have that ability to customize things on the fly. But when I got started in this business, I didn't have that opportunity. In fact, when I got started in this entire thing, it was funny. I was, man, I had to rewind back how many years ago. Probably 14, 15 years ago now. Man, I'm getting old. I look like I'm 11, but it was a long time ago, I swear. And I was doing internet marketing. I was learning some stuff and I got invited to go to an event. There was an email I saw. It was Armand Moran. He was doing an event called The Big Seminar that he did every year. And I think this was a big seminar, like three or four. He'd been doing it a couple of years. I saw people going and I was like, I want to go. It was kind of expensive. He had to pay money for the tickets and the flights and the hotels. And it was probably five grand when I was in, to be able to go and experience this. And I was just like, I don't really have that kind of money, but I want to go and learn internet marketing. A little did I know the journey it was going to take me on. So I get there. I think it was in Atlanta and I fly there and I get there the first day and I don't know anybody. I'm a nerdy kid in the back with my glasses on and my pad of paper and I'm taking notes trying to learn internet marketing. The first presenter gets up there and starts teaching. And the way the events used to run back then, you know it's fun, I can live. We don't do it this way. But back then, this is why they get the bad name. People call them pitch fest and stuff where every single speaker was selling something. And I didn't know this is what was happening. So the first speaker gets up, he's talking and like, I'm sucking in the story. It's all charismatic. It's awesome. And then he's, at the end, he like sells something. And I was kind of confused. I was like, wait, what's this guy doing? And he sells something. And I think it was a $2,000 package. And people start jumping up like popcorn in the audience, popping up and running to the back. And I was kind of, I was like having my notes and I was kind of confused. And I was like, what? And I was like, I'm looking back in the back of the room and I'm doing the math and I'm like $2,000 package. I'm like, two, four, six, eight, ten, twelve. I'm counting all the people and I was like, I think that guy did like $60,000 or $70,000 in sales right then. I was like, that was insane. And then the next guy gets up and he's selling like a $5,000 package. He does this thing and he sells it. And again, people are popping up like crazy running back and I'm like, five, ten, fifteen, twenty. Did like $120,000 in sales. And the next person came, next person, and kept going person after person after person for the entire three day event. And at the very end of this, this is very smart of Armand, the very end he had a guy get up on stage named John Childers. And John Childers is a public speaking coach. He teaches people how to sell stuff. And John's got a thick country accent, a southern accent and just, and he gets on stage and he was like, have you guys noticed what has been happening here today? And I'm just like, yes, I have. He's like, kind of goes in deeper about it. He's like, I want to talk to you about the most profitable business in the world. It's the business of public speaking. And I remember the time, if you guys, I wish I could put you guys in my state back then, twelve, fourteen years ago, whenever this experience was. But I was a shy, awkward kid. I had a buzz head. I had glasses on. I'm still very introverted. A lot of people don't believe me, but people who know me understand it. Like if you see me at Funalike Live event, I go on stage. I feel amazing. I love it all. I love being on stage. If you talk to me in the hallway 101, super uncomfortable, super awkward, super nervous. I just don't do well in those situations, right? And if you watch me back then, it's funny. This is before video was a big thing on the internet, but as soon as video came out four or five years later, I watched my old videos. I was so nervous. I didn't dare to look directly at the camera lens. That's how nervous I would look down. My videos would talk like this. And I wish I could show you, in fact, I'm gonna have to go find some clips to show you guys how awkward I was and how nervous. Because it'll give you guys hope. For those who were at Funalike Live event, you probably saw the same thing. Payne June gave his presentation. And Payne's one of the best stage presenters out there. And he showed a video of him when he was learning. He was awkward and nervous. And so put yourself in that situation. Me, this introverted kid who was an internet marketer because I didn't want to talk to people. I didn't want to get a real job. I was scared. I was like all these things. But I'm watching this process of people running to the back, hundreds of thousands, the millions of dollars made in the weekend. And I was like, I have to learn that skill. Like, if some dude can make $100,000 in an hour and a half, I have to learn that skill. But I'm like, but I'm scared of people. I'm scared. I don't dare step on stage. Like, I had, I talked too fast. I mumbled. I still have these issues. You probably noticed. I have a lot more energy now than I used to because I understand some things we'll talk about during this, during today. Anyway, that's kind of the state I was in. And John Childers, he's up there, he starts talking about this. He said, he said, I want to understand. He's like, there's two types, there's two speaking businesses out there. He's like, the first speaking business is you got to be famous. He's like, famous people. He started naming off people. I can't remember who was, you know, but, you know, Brad Pitt or Ashton Kutcher or Will Smith or whatever. He's like, they are famous people and they get paid to speak and they go out there and some of them paid $50,000 to come on stage and to give a 60 minute presentation or maybe $100,000. You get Oprah, it's like, you know, $300,000 for presentation. He's like, you have to be famous to be able to make money in that kind of business. He's like, there's a second speaking business. He's like, the second speaking business, he's like, you don't have to be famous at all. He's like, I'm not famous. Nobody knows who I am. I go to the grocery store, I walk around, nobody knows, nobody takes pictures with me, nobody knows who I am. He's like, I guess how much money I get paid to speak. And he's like, he's like, Brad Pitt or whatever gets paid $50,000. He's like, I want to speak for less than a hundred. And I was like, what? And he's like, but the difference is how we do it. And he's like, there's getting fees to go and speak or there's, you speak and you sell and you split the money with the promoter 50-50. He's like, these are the two models. Like what you guys saw here this weekend over the last three days is that second model. He's like, each of these speakers made more money than Oprah than Will Smith and any of these people make from a fee to speak because they understand this business of how it works. And I was just like mesmerized on the thing. I'm like, oh my gosh. And then John goes through this presentation and if you can find old John Childers, I found a bunch of stuff on eBay and I might contact him see if I can republish some of this stuff. It's so good. But anyway, he does this whole presentation and he's selling a $20,000, learn how to speak from stage program. And it was really, really cool. And I'm going to tell you his clothes because it was like the coolest clothes ever. So he gets, and this is just story time, but hopefully some of you guys are going to take this and use this because it's really, really powerful. I've never actually used this as a clothes, but it, man, it works so good. So the $20,000 speaking program. And he said, but this is the way it's going to work. He said, well, the way it's going to work is you're going to pay me $5,000 today and that'll get you into the program. Then you're going to come to my office. We're going to train you for five days how to be a public speaker and how to do pitching from the stage and closing and how to create a product and offer all sorts of stuff like that. And then after that, then I'm going to put you on the road. I'm going to connect you with promoters and things like that. And you're going to go on the road. You're going to speak and sell. And he's like, and then what you're going to do is 50% of all the money you earn from the stage from that point forward, you're going to send to me until I've been paid the last $15,000 you stole me. Okay. And so he, and so it's basically $5,000 down. He's going to train you on the skill, send you out there. And if the money you make, you'll pay him back the other $15,000 you owe him. And then he talked like, I'm going to see if I can find some of these old pitches for you guys. They're so good. And so a pitch was like, you know how I know you're going to be successful? Cause you've got my money. You've got $15,000 you owe me. And I'm going to make sure you're successful cause you've got my money. And if you don't make that money, I don't get it. So I'm going to make sure you get, you learn how to pitch. Hey, you got to get off or you can go out on the stage and it creates so much belief. It was, it was crazy. Now at the time I'd spent my last $5,000 to come to Armand's event. So I couldn't afford the 20, but he still almost closed me. But it was like, it opened up this window to me. It was like my big epiphany of just like, I have to learn this skill. I've got to do it. And so I remember getting back home afterwards and I was like, I got to learn public speaking. I learned how to sell from the stage. I saw it happen. I saw John Childers and in my head I'm like, but, but these people are doing it wrong. I was like, all those guys, like I went there and learned internet marketing. I didn't learn internet marketing. Like he's like, I got some good stuff. I didn't learn anything. I was like, I could teach way better. People give me more to me, more money because I can teach better. I thought that was the secret, right? And so I remember a couple months later, I got invited to speak at an event and I was just like, this is it. This is my chance. And the guy was the promoter. I was like, this is how it works. You come, you pay your own way, you pay or fly your own hotel, you get 90 minutes to your presentation. At the end of it, you can sell something and then we split the money, 50, 50, whatever you sell. And so I was like, okay, that makes sense. Cool. And I told him, I'm like, I'm like, I am so much better than any of your speakers. I know more internet marketing and more stuff. Like I'm going to destroy these guys. And so I go out there to do this presentation and, and I fly out and I remember my slides was just like detailed, like step by step. First do this and I like, I get there for, for an hour and a half. I'm just teaching everything I got. And I see people there, it's like taking notes, like craze and blowing their minds. And the end, I still remember like, there's probably a hundred people are selling their mind. I remember I had a slide up and I was like, with the product I was going to sell, I was like, I'm not going to insult your intelligence like the other speakers are going to do. I'm just going to tell you what this is worth because I know what it's worth and you've seen the value of what I offer. So what I'm going to sell you is this thing and it's a thousand dollars. And then I know what to do. Typically like now I train the audio visual team. Like when I done crank music up, we need energy going so people are flowing and all sorts of stuff. We'll talk about, we'll talk about the process of stage selling versus webinars here in a couple hours. But I didn't know how to coach the people through it from the thing. And so like it happens and it's just like dead quiet silence. Nobody moves. And I'm off. I'm like, well, that's it. The end. Okay. And I awkwardly kind of start walking off stage and there's like a couple of people are like clapping. And I'm like, God, and I walk off stage and people come out, all the presentations are amazing. I have like 30 pages of notes. I'm like, why didn't you buy? And like, well, I got so much to go do. Like I don't need it now. Like I got everything I need. Like I'm good. And I was like, but there's like, I only like scratch. There's so much more like you need to do to be able to. And I like, yeah, I just, I got a lift to do. And it was awkward. I walked out and I remember all the other speakers are on the hallway. How'd you do that? Cause they all, it's like, it's like sports, right? Like you win a, you win a wrestling match. Everyone's like, how did you do it? Like what was the score? Same thing. I come out in the hallway as speakers. How'd it go? I'm like, not good. But like, how many clothes? I'm like, I don't think any of that. You didn't close any. I'm like, yeah. I'm going to go to the bathroom real quick. And so I snuck off and I literally, I assume you guys heard the story before I ran the elevator, go to my room and I locked myself in my room for the next three days. Cause I was so humiliated. I didn't want the other speakers to know that I bombed. I didn't want to see him. I didn't want to hear from him. And I was just like, this was the worst feeling I've ever felt in my life. And for three days I sat in my room and I watched every movie on the movie channel that was rated anywhere from G to PG 13. And then I ordered Hagen Dazs, ice cream and coconut shrimp. And I sat there for three days and that's all I did. Super embarrassed to go out. And I remember saying to myself, I will never do this again. That was the worst experience. Like it was embarrassing. Like no. And I didn't for a long time. And then fast forward a little while later, this guy named Alex Mendozian, who's still a friend and a mentor. He came out with a course called Tell Seminar Secrets. And it was like similar to this whole selling from stage thing that suffers on the phone. And I was like, okay, maybe I can do that. So I bought the course, 1,000 bucks, listened to it, started doing Tell Seminars and started making a bunch of money. And my Tell Seminars weren't scripted at all. I'd get on the line and either I would talk for an hour or I'd interview someone for an hour and then in an hour and I would be like, okay, hey, do you have a special offer in person? Like, yeah, I got this package. You can buy it for $300 and go to this website. And it started working. I didn't make a ton of money. I started making some money, started making sales. And it was really, really cool. I was doing Tell Seminars and I was learning how to speak a little bit. And it wasn't awkward because I had to like stand on stage and for 9,000 people I was on the phone. In fact, one time I was doing a Tell Seminar. I started getting more too comfortable. I was sitting in my seat and I was leaning back, had my feet on the table and I'm interviewing this guy who had a product to sell. And I'm sitting there. All of a sudden the chair slipped out from underneath me and I totally fall on my back in the middle of the interview. And he's like, you okay now? I'm like, yeah, just my trash can knocked over and I was trying to get up and so embarrassing. But I was more comfortable. I started learning this process. And then fast forward, I don't know, six or seven months later, what I would do is I had a little bit of a list of the times. I invited a list of Tell Seminar to introduce him to a speaker and the speaker would speak for an hour and then sell something at the end for like 5 or 10 minutes. And I had this guy named Mike Chen and Mike came on and he said, hey, I want to do a Tell Seminar with you. But he's like, but I figured out, like I've scripted a little bit where if you follow the script, what will happen is people like it dramatically increases how many people buy. And I was like, oh my God, it's like doing Q&A. He's like, no, he's like, trust me. Like, he's like, these are the questions you need to ask me. It'll set up, it'll resolve people's concerns. So when we get to the sell at the end, all their concerns are gone. I was like, okay. So he's like, yeah, any question you want, but make sure you ask me these. I can't remember three or four questions. So I remember the questions. One of them was like, the thing he was selling was called Make Your Own Software. It's a software program that made software. It's one of the questions. And there was like, hey, Mike, a lot of people who tried to hire programmers in the past and paid $4,000 or $5,000 to create a very simple software program. Why is it that you priced Make Your Own Software at only $300? That seems super cheap. And I read that, I was like, dude, I'm not going to read this. He's like, you have to read that. I'm like, why? Because right now, every single person thinks that $300 is a lot for this software. He's like, but if we can reframe their mind, we're all of a sudden they think, oh yeah, like $300 is cheap. It does cost $5,000 to hire a real programmer. Then all of our price objections will disappear when we get to the end. I was like, huh. And he had four or five questions like that that were all his major concerns. He said, ask this question, I'll resolve it, and we get to the end. That question will no longer be a concern for someone. Just like five, seven, one on one, right? I'd be like, so what's your concern? You think it's too expensive? Well, actually, I could resolve that specific concern. So Mike had done so many tell seminars. He knew what the main concerns people had. And so he crafted the presentation that way, crafted my questions. So I resolved the concerns through the content section. And then we got to the close. There was no barrier resistance. And so he did that. And that tell seminar was the very first one I made over, what's five figure, $10,000. I was over $10,000, over five figures on a tell seminar. And I was just like, what? This is insane. So then I started like, when I would bring a tell seminar guest on, or if I'd go on somebody else's to sell my product, I would specifically like, OK, what are the big concerns people have? And I would ask questions to make sure we knock down those concerns along the way. Right? Like I'm a fast learner. When money, when more money pops out the back end, I try to reverse engineer to do that more often. Like that's been my whole life the last 15 years. More money came out. What did I do to create more money? Let me tweak that so the next time I can create even more money, right? And that's the whole process. And so I'm doing that tell seminars. It's working. And then a little while later, this weird thing called webinars came out. We were one of the first people to really put GoToWebinar to the test. And we started webinars. I think the first year that GoToWebinar came out, we did like 80 or 90 webinars. Did over a million dollars in sales, interviewing people, doing the same thing, the same thing as I was crafting those things. About that time, I decided I was going to take a second whack at like speaking at seminars, right? Because I'm like, I feel more comfortable. I've done phones, done some tell seminars, some webinars. And so I started contacting people that had events going on. I said, hey, can I come speak at your event? And some knew who I was. And I said, you know, I have a really good offer I can sell. And so I started going to some seminars. What was cool is I'd go to these events. And I'd sit there for three or four days at the events. And I would watch all these other people who are really, really, really good stage presenters. And I would watch them. And I would take notes on what they were doing. Like, man, look how we did that. Oh, like that story was so cool. And I started studying what they were doing. And I would sit there for the entire event, taking notes, not on what they were teaching, but on how they were doing it, how they were setting up the sell, how they were crafting their offers, like all these things. And it was like the greatest college education in my life. And I did this for about two years. I was on the road speaking and selling and doing these things. But more so it was me learning from these other speakers. And back then, I mean, it was like the business model for so many years was speaking and selling. And then, anyway, so I did that for about two years. I remember one day, after two years of doing this, I had twin boys at the time that were super young. I think my daughter was either born or about to be born. And I was sitting in the Boise airport about to flat some other event. It was like 12.30 at night. I was the only person in the airport. I remember sitting there and I remember texting my wife. I felt so miserable. I was just like, to go on the road again. I remember texting my wife. I was like, I was like, hey, I'm going to quit. And she said, quit what? I'm like, I'm going to quit speaking. I just, I'm tired of this traveling. This wasn't, this wasn't doing it for me. And she's like, you can't, that's how we feed our kids. And I was like, I know, but like, I think I can replicate this online. And and after that, I told the promoters like I'm done. And I ended up kind of quitting. I didn't speak again from stage for five or six years or maybe even longer. It was a long time. But I went back and started crafting this stuff through webinars and that's the story from the other day. But that's kind of what happened. I quit doing the road, but I came back and started perfecting this more and more online. But one of the stresses when I was on the road and I was learning this stuff, like I just, I learned from so many amazing people and so many little pieces. And so if you read the expert seekers book, I tried to give credit to people for the pieces I learned by sharing so many of those stories in here. I'm going to share a lot of them with you guys as well because they're essential to set up what we're trying to do in this training. But everyone I learned different things. And and so like I learned trial closes from Ted Thomas. They called him the Pied Piper. Like Ted, if you see him like a Robert Kiyosaki event, he'd be like walking. If you Google like Ted Thomas Pied Piper, you'll see videos of him. And there he's like standing in front holding his credit card. There'll be a line of like 300 people with their credit cards in hand, like following him to the back of the room. He taught me about trial clothes. I learned from Armand Moran this thing called the stack. I'm going to walk you through the stack because it's the most important, it's a powerful thing in the entire webinar. Jason Flattle taught me how to break and rebuild belief patterns. Like all these people I learned stuff from and they all kind of from that kept building things got better and better and better at it. Until a couple of years ago, we kind of took all these things and put it into a process to call a perfect webinar, which I'm going to be walking us through today. But that's kind of the journey I went on to get this stuff. So it's not something I just made up yesterday. It's not something I just figured out. It's been time tested by me for over a decade for other people for even longer. What's interesting about this is the whole public speaking thing was big. And then when the whole economy crashed in 2008 or whatever, the one for me and events happening two or three times a week, they all disappeared. And for four or five years, events were gone. And so most of the guys who were masters at this have disappeared. I go to events nowadays and it shocks me how bad most people are from selling a stage. It is insane. I don't think that I am special other than the fact that I've continued to do it. When the events stopped, I did it online for five or six years and they came back. I'm the only one. I spoke to the first grand cardon event. It was funny. I'm going to tease him about this a little bit. But he called me ahead of time. He's like, okay, how are you, come back to your presentation. I kind of explained it to him. He's like, no, no, no. He's like, that's not going to work. I've worked with webinar guys like you, like Lewis Howes and other people. He's like, don't do any of that stuff. He's like, get on stage. Tell people what your money costs. They're going to buy it because they love you and then teach for an hour. I'm like, okay. And I told him, sure, I'll definitely do that. And I was like, we hung up and I'm like, I'm not doing that. I'm doing my work. And I told him, okay, when we got there, I was like, okay, this is what we need to do. We need to organize them. We need to do the tables here and here. There's a way to set up the room, a whole bunch of stuff that we're going to talk about today. This is what we need to need. And Grant and staff is like, sorry, man, we just don't have time for that. We don't have those resources here. Just tell people to go to a website and buy them. I'm like, no, dude. Luckily for me, they gave me a little leeway because they wanted, like, after you get done and Grant's come up on stage and interview, I'm like, no, that'll destroy my cells. Trust me. I've done this for a long time. Just trust the process. But they had never seen someone who understood the art of this business, right? Because the business literally just kind of died. And so finally, he kind of was like, fine, Grant, we're going to interview you. We'll give you 20 minutes afterwards to close cells. There's no sales tables, but you can have guys at these two doors. So I had Dave Woodward sitting next to the door with a stack of order forms in a box. And luckily, Alex and Layla Hormozzi were in the audience as attendees and I'm friends with them. So they stood at the door with forms and pens. And I did the clothes and all of a sudden, if you went to the first 10 X, you see this rush of people flooding to the doors. And Grant and his team were just like, what the crap? And we had all these people, Dave's pulling out forms out of a box handing him out with pens that we had. It was just a huge, awkward thing. And it was so poorly choreographed. We still did a million dollars in sales. Had we choreographed it? I think we could have done a lot more. But anyway, it is what it is. And afterwards, Grant pulled me aside in the back room. He's like, I've never seen someone do something like that before. And I was like, I've seen it a lot. That's how I know how to do it. But the art is dead. People aren't doing it anymore. Anyway, that's kind of what happened. And then fast forward now a couple months later, he's like, hey, we're doing an event. There's going to be 9,000 people out of here you want to speak at. And I was like, yes. And I was like, but can I choreograph at this time? And he's like, you can do whatever you want. He's like, you tell us whatever you want. We will make it happen. And I was like, OK. And so this time, they let us choreograph the whole thing from the room, the setup, the layout, and all the stuff I'm going to be walking us through today. And as you know, we were able to execute and do over $3 million in sales in 90 minutes. And it was really, really cool. And so I'm telling you this because it's not anything special. Like, again, I don't think I'm the best stage presenter in the world. I'm just one of the few who still do it. It's a dead art. It's a skill that people understand. When you understand it, though, it's the most powerful thing in the world. OK. I don't care how good of a salesperson you are selling one-on-one. There's none of you guys that can do $3 million in 90 minutes because you can't talk to enough people. OK. I guarantee most of you guys are better closers than me. You can out close one-on-one. Like you have higher closing percentage, all those types of things. I guarantee you have it. But what I have is difference. I can close 10, 20, 100, 1,000, 9,000 people all at once. Now, while the $3 million in 90 minutes is cool, what a lot of you guys don't know, the second half of this story, is exactly 30 days later we had our event, 30 days to the day. In fact, I wore the same clothes that presentation as I wore on the 10X one. And I'm wearing today just because it's for good luck, right? But 90 days later, I did a presentation in front of 3,000 people that did over $10 million. So $3 million was the record till 30 days later when we broke it again. So this process is powerful. It's not magic. It's just it's a dead art. And if you guys learn this art, you can do it in any business you want. We've got chiropractors now who are doing this process. Where instead of going out and doing all the stuff to lead you, and they're getting people in the room together, they do the perfect webinar, they're closing people and bringing them back and signing up 10, 20, 30 clients at once. I have a 12-year-old kid who has been doing this in school. He teaches his, Stephen Larson's interview yesterday. And he was telling me he teaches his lessons based, he teaches class now based on the perfect webinar script. There's people doing services, and through supplements, physical products. You can do selling cars. This process works everywhere. It's just shifting your mindset from one-on-one selling to mass selling. And when you understand the nuances of how this game works, it's amazing. So the same way that most of you guys have sold one-on-one, 10,000 hours or 100,000 hours. And you know, I know the questions, the concerns, how they're gonna come up, how I'm gonna resolve it. That's what I have right here. I've done 10,000 hours of this stuff. So I know how to resolve all concerns, but so by the time I present this, irresistible offer, people have to say yes. Okay, and so that's what we're walking yesterday. It's something that's super learnable, it's easy, and it's a lot of fun. Okay, so that's the setup of what's gonna be happening over the next few hours, and I'm excited for it. So, all right. But before we get to the webinar, so I'm gonna have a session today, or this morning I wanna talk about first, and we're gonna break, and I'm gonna come back, and I'm actually gonna diagram out the whole perfect webinar process, because that's the key, but there's some things that happen ahead of time, okay? And it comes down to, most of these things are in the beginning of the expert seeker's book. If you understand these things, and you get these things set up correctly, it makes this way easier. If you do these things wrong, it's like pushing a rock up a hill. It's really, really hard. And so it comes down to picking the right audience, creating the right offer, understanding what your new opportunity, all those kinds of things. If you understand those things right, it makes everything else easier. So I'm gonna spend this first session talking about those kind of things, because if we get that right, everything else becomes easy. So this whole session is made about creating an irresistible offer. Okay, all right. Now, some context for this, and I said this stuff, I'm really excited. I got a whiteboard here, so I'm gonna be sketching, and they're gonna be flipping back and forth, see this, can see me sketch out. I can kind of sketch out these concepts as you're going through it. But if you read the expert seeker's book, in the very beginning of it, I talk about a concept of creating a movement, or a mask, and whatever it is, right? Like, again, if we're doing group selling, I'm trying to get a whole bunch of people to move with me somewhere, right? If you're trying to build something where people buy from you over and over and over again, which all business should be focusing on. If you're selling a transactional-based business, man, you gotta start shifting your mind, because the reason why I can do $3 million and do $10 million 30 days later is because I've created a movement of people that are following me, and they buy it, and they continue to buy it. And people, there's this really cool, I learned this from Dan Kennedy. He said that people will continue to buy until one of two things happen. Either number one, you stop selling them stuff, or number two, you offend them. Okay, so if you treat people as good as humanly possible, and you don't offend them, and you keep making really good offers, people will continue to buy. And so if you're a business where a transactional ends once, like, start thinking differently about your business. All businesses can do this. I've seen pizza shops do this. I've seen car dealerships do this. I've seen everyone, like, you've got to shift from, like, a transactional thing to, like, what are the results I'm trying to get for somebody? And so, again, that's like a class in and of itself, but just thinking through that. So the first thing I want to talk about is, in the Expert Seekers book, I talk about how to create a movement, and there's three-quart components to any mass movement. And so I'm going to draw out this little triangle right here. For those who've done your homework and read the Expert Seekers book, you kind of know about this. Now, what's interesting is I was doing the study for this book and really trying to understand movements. I study a lot of different things. I study cults, like, how cults were built, right? I study really negative movements, like the Nazi Party and leaders on the bad side of things, right? And I look at things on the really, really good side of things, like Christianity and, you know, other things. And I studied all sorts of things, everything from, again, cults to business to, like, religion and everything in between. And what was interesting is I was going through this deep dive process of study. Like, there was a pattern that happened in every single one of these instances. And I'm a big pattern guy, because as soon as you can figure out what the pattern is, as soon as you can identify it, then you can amplify it, right? Identify a pattern and amplify it. If you're not aware of it, it's hard to amplify it, right? So I'm always identifying the pattern. So if you look at, I mean, all my books, if you've read them, or if you can see up there, there's always doodled out all the patterns, right? Like, always doodled out the pattern when I find it. And so for this is, like, this is the pattern of how all mass movements throughout time have been created. Now, if I want to create any kind of movement, small movement, medium movement, if I want to do more of my family with my religion with, like, whatever it is you're trying to do, it doesn't matter, the process is the same, OK? I'm trying to create a movement within my family right now. It's the same kind of things, right? So if you look at this, the first thing, every mass movement, they have, I like to call, I like to call it an attractive character or charismatic leader, right? There's somebody there that people can identify with, OK? So there's that. Now, in most of these movements, especially for you guys who are doing this process, it's usually going to be you or someone on your team or maybe it's a success story, whatever, but there's a person that's associated with it, right? And it's happening more and more nowadays. Back, you know, 100 years ago, people would identify and associate with the brand. Nowadays, it's different. People don't associate with brands nearly as much as they associate with humans, right? We're in a connection economy, like, we connect with people. And so most good businesses, there's a human being tied into it. And that's what causes people to stick is the human, it's the attractive characters, the charismatic leader, the person they can plug into and be like, that person is going to take me where we're going, right? ClickFunnels is a software company. Why are we beating out all the other software companies in our space? Besides the fact our software is the best, like, there's connection. People feel connected to me, right? They read my books, they understand my stories, like, there's connection there. Or even, like, let's say some other software comes out with a different feature or it's cheaper or whatever, right? Because there's connection, people aren't leaving, OK? So every movement has that. And I'm not going to go deep into that today. That's one of the things. Number two is all of them have a future-based cause, OK? So future-based cause is like, where are we going? Every mass movement, there's a cause. Like, we're going this direction, guys. This is where we're collectively going as a group, OK? That's why people, they buy into something, right? Think about the movements that you're part of. Think about whatever religious thing you believe in, OK? Is there a future-based thing? Are you all working towards something? Think about the businesses. Think about software. Think about, like, whatever it is you're part of, OK? Click phones with tons of future-based cause. If you read the book, I'm not going to go deep into it right now, but if you read the book, like, I share a bunch, and I share the process of how we created our future-based cause, OK? And the third one, one I'm going to spend one more time on today, is that they always offer somebody a new opportunity. And this right here is the key to creating an offer, is the new opportunity, OK? Now, real quick, if I go deeper into a new opportunity, I want to bring this up again for a couple reasons. And the main one is, as I show you guys the webinar at 10X, I want you guys to identify these things. Like, how is Russell bringing up his attractive character, OK? Like, I come out immediately, and I don't do it in a bragging way. A lot of people, they mistake this when they get on stage and they spend 20 minutes talking about how great they are. Like, I was on Fox and CNN and today's show, I'm like, oh, like, all that kind of crap. And all it does is that posturing, it just pushes you away from people. Watch as I tell my stories, how I try to bring people together with me. I become vulnerable, it connects people with me, OK? A lot of times we think that you as the attractive character is you telling people why you're better than them and it's not. It's pivoting, it's positioning, we're saying, this is something amazing, this is why you should care who I am, but let me explain why I'm just like you. Like, that's the connection with the attractive character. All comes back to that, right? So notice that as I show you guys the presentation, like, where am I weaving in my attractive character where now you actually care about my outcome, right? Number two is the future-based cause. Notice how throughout here, I'm talking about, like from slide number two, you guys will notice, I'm going future-based cause, I'm talking about the four-minute mile, I'm talking about my four-minute mile, I'm talking about all these things, I'm talking about two comma club, and two comma club X, like, this is where we're going, like from the very beginning, before people even know the movement they're part of, I'm talking about the future where we're going and people are buying that immediately. And then I'm able to come and present this new opportunity that then that's the key, that's, they offer at the end is what makes this movement happen, OK? So there's the character, that's you, and there's so much stuff we talk about developing that. There's the character, there's a future-based cause, and then there's new opportunities. So this is happening inside the webinar as well. If you look at, like, the webinar process, like, it's following this, it's like, boom, here's Russell introducing himself, here's him introducing the future-based cause we're all part of, and then now I'm going to make this new opportunity, boom, which now makes this thing this movement, with like, I'm in this movement. OK, if you try to make it cause a movement, and you only have one of the three here, it's going to tip over. It's like a table with only two legs, like, you just can't stand up, right? So you have all three of them, OK? So just be aware of that and notice that. And if you want to go deeper into these first two, read the book, if you haven't read the book yet, because it goes a lot deeper into that. All right, so new opportunities. So this is what we're talking about, because this is what comes down to creating the offer that we're going to be selling people at the end of it, OK? Because if you have a really good offer, even if your presentation sucks, you're going to sell it, right? And if you have a great presentation and a great offer, that's when you hit $3 million in 90 minutes, right? Like, it's a blend of those two things. But if your opportunity sucks, even if your presentation's good, if your offer's horrible, no one's going to buy, OK? So it starts here, like, figuring out this piece first, is what is the new opportunity? Now, one of the biggest mistakes that people make, and I talk about this a lot in the book as well, is when they create a product, an offer, a thing that they're selling people, what they traditionally do is they create what we call an improvement offer. OK, improvement offer means you're going to help people improve. They're going to become better, faster, stronger, smarter. Usually there's an ER after an improvement offer. OK, and the problem with improvement offers is a lot of them. One of the biggest problems with improvement offer is for somebody to say, I want to improve, they have to admit that they suck at something. OK, in us humans, we have this weird thing with pride and all sorts of things where it's really hard for us to say, I'm really bad at that thing. So if them say, I'm going to become better, I say, I suck at this thing, therefore I'm going to become better. And so we have this emotional turmoil inside of it. I, do I want to admit that I'm horrible? Like, I don't know if I do or not. It's just a huge thing, and it's difficult, right? And so you're fighting against that. Number two is a lot of people have desires, right? Like, they have desire to be better, to be smarter, but to improve, it doesn't take just desire, it takes ambition, OK? Now while everybody has a desire, I'd say maybe 2% to 3% of the population actually has ambition. And so the only people who are going to say yes to an improvement offer are people who actually have ambition. Does that make sense? And so if you look at like my value ladder, which we're not going to talk about today, but, the back of my value, the most expensive stuff I'm selling, I'm selling improvement, right? Because I'm getting the people with ambition, because they're willing to spend $100,000 to work with me, right? So on the back of the value ladder, I have ambition, but for the most part, a new opportunity, I want to create a new opportunity that's desire. So if I'm creating something that's an improvement offer, again, I'm only going to get the people who are ambitious, who take it, which is not the masses, OK? And so instead, I got to create a new opportunity. So what's a new opportunity? A new opportunity is something completely different. If I'm going to do a new opportunity, the nice thing is I don't have to say, oh, I suck at this, therefore, I'm going to make a new opportunity. You just basically come back saying, look, that thing you tried in the past is horrible. In fact, I'm going to draw this. This will, the way you can figure out new opportunities, this is the easiest way, is look at this. Like, here's a little mountain right here, right? And here is the result that your person wants to get. There's a little flag. Yay! OK? And so they're down here, and they're looking up the mountain, and they're looking up here and saying, that's what I want. I want to lose weight. I want to make money. I want a new car. I want to, like, whatever. Like, this is the result they want, right? OK. Now, the problem is that if they are coming to you, my guess, for most of you guys who are at the 10X event, or you've heard me speak, like, you're, like, you didn't know that your goal was to build funnels, right? All you do is I want to grow my company. I want to 10X my company. I want to make more money, right? And so there's a lot of ways you can do that, right? And so there's all these different vehicles. So here's a little car. Here's a little car right here, right? And there's another car. And there's a whole car parking lot. And we've got a lot of car people here in this training today. So there's a whole bunch of cars out there, right? And they're probably in a car right now, right? They drove in a car to get to the car lot, right? Like, you have your business. You're already selling something. You got to sell something, whatever you're doing something. So that's the vehicle you're in. You came to the 10X event in your car, and you're getting there. All of a sudden, speakers come up, and every single speaker is presenting you guys a new car. And they're like, that car you're in right now, it sucks. It's not going to get you your result. You want a 10X, not going to happen, okay? But I've got a car that's way better. Get out of your car. Come into my Ferrari. This Ferrari over here, this car is better. It's faster, stronger, and it'll get you your result way faster, okay? That was the presentation to you. My funnel is just a Ferrari. You're all trying to 10X your business anyway. You learn from all the other speakers. They all gave you, they all made you an offer, an opportunity, right? And I said, look, I believe that the fastest way to 10X your business is through funnels. They're free to jump into my car. Get rid of whatever you're doing and come into my new vehicle. This is the new opportunity that I'm presenting to you. Does that make sense? And so you have that new opportunity. All of a sudden, you're like, oh my gosh. And what's cool about it, because it's a new opportunity, you don't have any, it's not like, I can come and I can make fun of all the stuff you tried in the past. If you fail because whatever it is, the vehicle you're in right now, it's the wrong vehicle. It's not a good thing. So I can make fun of that as opposed to making fun of you. And improve and offer you to make fun of yourself. Like I'm really bad at this. If I'm gonna improve, I have to do this other thing, right? Here I can say, look, the thing you're trying, that's the problem, like websites are dumb. Hiring a sales team is dumb. Whatever like the thing you're doing right now, like this is the answer. This is the new opportunity that I'm offering you. It's the grass is green on the other side of the pasture. It's a whole new opportunity you don't know about. You're unaware of it. If you try that out, it's gonna get you the thing fastest. So if you don't know what your new opportunity is, couple things. I want you to map this out and say, okay, here's my dream customer, right? Here's my dream customer, they're sitting right here. This is the result I'm trying to offer them. I'm trying to offer them a new car, a new life, a new health plan, a new, like whatever it is you're selling them. So look at that, this is the result you're trying to get them. And the first thing I want you to ask is, what vehicle are they currently in trying to get that result? What's the vehicle that they're in? They're doing something, right? If you're weight loss, my guess is that the time they come to you, it's not the first time they try to lose weight. In fact, Katelyn Polin told me that the average woman does eight weight loss plans a year. Eight, it's almost one a month, okay? So if you're not coming back, hey guys, that's a really cool thing. I'm gonna teach you how to cut calories. And like, nobody, like that's what they've already been trying. So she came into them and said, okay, this is a vehicle a year and you're in the like how to lose weight category and you're doing things, if I can be like, I've got a better way to do that. Instead of doing that, what we're gonna do is instead of doing this, we're gonna change it to this and we're gonna, it's gonna make it a little bit better for you. They're like, I'm already in the car, I'm already trying stuff, I'm not gonna give you money because like maybe it's a little better, I don't know. But she comes back and says, look, the reason why you failed at this point is because the vehicle you're in, like that's what's wrong, it's broken. Yesterday I was funnel hacking Drew, he's from fit to fat to fit. His whole thing is like, hey, I was a personal trainer and I was really fit and I never knew how to lose weight. I was telling people how to do it. And then I was like, well, if I'm gonna lose weight, I gotta, if I'm gonna teach people to lose weight, I gotta lose it too. So we went and gained like 100 pounds and got really, really heavy. And then he lost all, so it went fit to fat to fit, right? And I was like, and I learned the process of like, everything I was trying was wrong. Like this is what actually the secret is. And it's this thing called for him as ketosis, like oh, so then you ketosis, that's the vehicle, this thing gives you the result. And also you present some of that new opportunity and they're like, oh my gosh, I haven't failed because of me, I've failed because of the vehicle was wrong. I wanna get in the Ferrari and when you make that opportunity, the opportunity to switch, that's the key. You're switching them from one opportunity to the other. So that's the key when you guys are making an offer, when you're doing a webinar, when you're doing a sound from stage, whatever it is, the biggest thing you understand is you are making them a new opportunity or giving them an opportunity to switch. So you have to figure out what is the opportunity that they're currently in, what's the vehicle that they're trying to get that result and what are you switching them into? All I did when I came to 10X, when I come to any time I speak, I'm looking at what are they trying to do right now to get the result? They're here because they wanna make money online, they're here because they wanna grow their business, they're here because they're trying to do whatever. I'm just trying to switch them, prove to them that whatever thing they're trying to do to get that result sucks and they switch them. This is the opportunity. And if I can make them that switch, then I got them, 100% in, line hook and sinker, is that what they say? And that's the key, okay, is the opportunity to switch. So that's kinda the first thing I wanna talk about here. Hopefully that makes sense to you guys and this takes some thinking for most of you guys. Most of you guys are selling the improvement offer right now. That's why you're struggling. You try to sell improvement offer on stage and you close one or two percent of the audience. You wanna close 10%, 20%, 50%, 100%, like you have to make an opportunity to switch. You're switching them from, this is how they're currently trying to get that result to this is the new opportunity, okay? You wanna study deeper on new opportunity, I talk about in the expert seekers book, I go deep in there, but that's the key. So spend some time figuring that out and every time you figure it out, you're like, is that an improvement offer? Was it worth it at the end? Is it better? Is it smarter as a fast store? Or is this thing, no, that sucks, get out, come over here. Okay, like one thing I'm gonna add on top of this. This is kinda cool, hope I can sketch it over here. Inside the expert seekers book, I talk about this concept of there's three core markets, right, there are, so there's, let's see, I'm gonna do health, wealth, and relationship. So it's health, wealth, and relationships. In fact, Stephen Larson was doing a presentation that I talked about this, and he called them three desires, which was kinda cool too, like the three core markets, three core desires. So you have to understand, whatever it is you're selling, it fits into one of these three things. Either people are buying it for their health, for their wealth, or their relationships. It works for everything. You guys are like, I'm a car dealership, I'm not selling any of that crap, like yes you are, okay? Why are people buying your car? Are they trying to save money? Maybe it's a wealth thing, are they trying to impress a girl? Maybe it's a relationship thing, maybe it's a family, there's different things, right? No matter what it is you're selling, it fits in one of these three core markets, or three core desires, or whatever you wanna call it, and think like, what's the desire? So that's the first thing, okay? Now if you look at, this is kind of like a history lesson of the markets for most people, okay? If you look at like when businesses began, back in the day, nobody was selling stuff, right? Everyone's kind of had their own farm, making their own food and their feeding, and then people started congregating, and things started shifting, and commerce was born, all sorts of stuff. And you figure out this, first person who figured this out was like, I don't know who it was, right? Let's just say it was whoever, right? And they're like, I'm gonna teach people, or I'm gonna create products to help people with their health, and they put out a product, and they were the first people there, okay? So what happens when you're the first person there, you're in this blue ocean, okay? So there's a book called, I thought it was called Red Ocean Blue Ocean, but it's not, Blue Ocean's something anyway, but it's a concept. So the first person in the market, they're in a blue ocean, right? And it's just them, and they're in the boat, they're throwing their thing in, they're reeling in, they're getting all this, they're pulling all this fish out, because it's just them, right? Your blue ocean, but what happens is as soon as you start pulling stuff out, then what happens is sharks start coming, they start looking around, like, oh my gosh, like that person's making a ton of money, teaching people how to do health. Like, well, I know how to do health, I'm gonna do it too, and then all of a sudden, boom, they come out, next person come out, next person come out, and soon there's like 10, 20, 100, 500,000 health gurus, right? And this blue ocean that you're swimming in, all of a sudden becomes like chopped up, bloody water, right? It's all bloody now. And now it's like, now you're competing with 20, 30, 40, 50 other people on this thing, right? That's hard. And everyone's throwing a bunch of improvement offers, okay? Improvement offers are what make this thing so bloody. Everyone's doing improvement offers, right? Then the same thing happens in relationship, starts blue and then it gets really, really bloody, and then wealth starts blue and it gets really, really bloody. So as soon as like, oh man, like it's hard to compete here now, because everybody's gonna have the same dollar, same human, same customers, everyone's trying to improve upon this stuff, and they're all making better mouth chats, but it's just kind of stuck in here, right? So then the next evolution in the market was like, well, people start breaking out, inside of this, inside of three markets, let me break out like, what are the three, or like, what's the sub market here? So inside of wealth, there's sub markets, right? So someone came off, well, there's a lot of ways to do wealth. I'm gonna teach you how to do wealth through real estate. Ooh. And so I'm gonna teach you how to do wealth through stock markets. I'm teaching wealth through internet marketing. Internet marketing, I'm teaching wealth by building a sales team. And also there's all these like, sub markets started coming out, right? And what's cool is sub markets came out, and instantly what happened, all these blue oceans, and all of a sudden, people started making a ton of money, right? Look at any of the people in any business you're in. And again, I talk a lot about information products, because it's really easy to see there, but it's true at any of them, right? Stephen Larson shared a really good example, with like Gillette, like Gillette razors, right? So you're out there shaving, what is that? Like it's a relationship thing, they sell sex, they sell relationships, like, so it's like, this is a relationship thing, like we're selling our razors to help you get better relationships, that becomes a sub market. And first, they're the only ones that get all the market share, and Austin, everyone else is like, oh, I want a piece of that, right? So razors, for example, is this beautiful blue ocean, at first, Gillette's making much money, and then like the Dollar Shave Club dude's like, I can make money in this, he makes a goofy video, puts up a thing, and does like, I don't know, have any hundreds of millions of dollars in sales, and it's getting bought for like over a billion dollars, right? But he's like, everyone's making money here, so he jumps in, and then another person, soon there's like 8,000 shaving companies, and guess what happens now? All of a sudden, that shaving thing goes from a blue ocean to a relatively red ocean, okay? So it was a sub market, shaving was a sub market. But in relationships, it was a sub market, so then all of a sudden like, the Dollar Shave Club guy does this, and all of a sudden this dude with a big old beard, Chris Stoikis, he looks over there and says, well, I can do Dollar Beard Club, because I think beards get you a better relationship. And Austin, he just creates this whole new blue ocean saying, the shaving guys are lame, and we're talking about red ocean, blue ocean, you're made up, he's like, the shaving guys are stupid, like, you don't want to be shaved, like men have beards, and so you launch a Dollar Beard Club, and it's zero to $12 million the first year, okay? Because you offer them a new opportunity. Don't be a wuss and shave, be a man and grow a beard, that's how you get real women, right? And Austin will launch this relationship thing over beards, okay, and then it's this blue ocean, people jump in, now it gets bloody, but like, that's the game, right? And so in most markets, the world that you're inheriting, right, is these are all bloody. You go one step deeper, these are bloody as well. So all these oceans here are now all bloody. And so where you haven't invested, where you guys have kind of inherited in the day and time that you live in this world is this, right? And so for you, the next step is it's time for you, you have to go figure out like which of the three desires, the three markets in my man, so let's say I'm in wealth, inside of wealth, which is the sub market I'm in, so like, okay, let's say I'm in real estate, inside of real estate, okay, now I need to create my own blue ocean inside of real estate, okay? This is the magic, this is the new opportunity, okay? This is the opportunity to switch, okay? This sub market right here are all of these sub vehicles, these vehicles that people are in. So you're coming in real estate saying, okay, this guy's teaching you flipping, this guy's doing this, this guy, like these are what people are doing. Now for you, you gotta figure out what's my new opportunity? What am I able to take, like what am I able to take and like switch them out of, right? And what's kind of fun about this, I'm gonna draw this one really red so you can see it, we come back to like some of the psychology, what I'll show you guys that I do in my presentation is when you're creating your blue ocean, right? Like so I told everyone in the world that funnels are the only way to 10X your business. So I'm over here in my blue ocean, they're like funnels are the greatest thing in the world, ah, and I'm having all this fun about it, right? And what I do is I look backwards to this sub market and I'm gonna throw rocks back at it, right? So here's me throwing a little rock, back of the sub market. So I'm like, how many of you guys think that, to 10X your business in the next 12 months, how many of you think you can do that by hiring a couple new sales people? Not gonna happen. How many of you guys think that 10X your business in the next 12 months, that it's gonna be a new sales script? Nope, not gonna happen, right? And I can be like, all the different vehicles that people are trying to do to get that result, I'm gonna throw rocks at them and make them just not real, not matter and like come with my new opportunity, like this is new opportunity, then it becomes the logical thing they have to switch to it, they're gonna get that result, okay? So that's kind of how the markets work. Let me see, I wanna make sure I covered all this. And so for you guys, what we're doing, we're creating this new opportunity, you're literally creating a blue ocean, like this is your new thing, it's unique to you, okay? You can go and do a me too thing, but I promise you you're gonna struggle because you're fighting in a red ocean. If you wanna take a business in literally 10X it's like, I gotta be different, I gotta be unique, like what am I switching people to? What's the opportunity that I'm offering that nobody else is? Okay, and this is not something you're gonna be like, oh, I got it right now. Think through this tonight, this weekend, over next week, over next month, like talk to your team about like, what is our new opportunity we're offering our customers? What is that? If you don't know what that is, then you're in tons of trouble. If you don't have anything yet, like now it's time to create it. Like what am I switching them into? Okay, what is your selling? What your mission, what your movement is? Like this is the key, is figuring out that new opportunity, okay? All right. Okay, cool. So now let me figure that out. Like this is my blue ocean, this is my new opportunity. Now I'm gonna convert that into something that's tangible, something I can actually sell, right? So let's just look at my business, for example. So it's plugged me into this business. I'm gonna plug me in maroon, because that's the odd color here, okay? So here's Russell. Yay, I'm the attractive character. I drink my old Kool-Aid, I figure out a thing, I want a story, a path, and I share my story. People bond with me, they're like, Russell's awesome, whatever. Like, he's figured this thing out, I believe in it. Here's my future-based cause, we're getting, everyone who's, whatever that, trying to get into Tucal Club, they become millionaires, and everyone Tucal Club, trying to get into Tucal Club X, and make $10 million in a funnel. This is where we're going, you're only just one funnel away, like all my future-based cause, I'll leave that all in there, and I'm like, yes, this is the thing, there's a lot of ways to 10X your business, you can do this, this, and this, but all those things are gonna be hard. The only way to get you to 10X your business in the next 12 months is the thing I'm gonna show you guys today. And here's where you guys will see our price marinade and the thing. Our price marinade, I only get too deep into it, but basically I get everybody in the audience to raise their hands and say, if I can get you to believe that the only way to 10X your business in the next, by this time next year, is by funnels, will you guys give me $11,552? And every audience raise their hands and say, yes, so if you can convince me that this is the right, the only vehicle to get me to my goal, I will give you $11,552 to do that for me. I get everybody to commit that in the first, I don't know, five or six minutes of my presentation. That was the thing I was scared about, that worked really, really good. And now I just gotta make, okay, well here's the opportunity to switch, and I switch it for them, and then boom, they've got it. So now I'm gonna take my new opportunity for me, is showing the only way to 10X your business in the next 12 months is by using funnels, okay, that was the theme of the event, so 10X, funnels, the only way to do that. Now what is the offer I'm gonna make them that actually makes that possible, okay? And so, hope I can get this all fit in here, that's about the top of my line there. So I'm now taking this new opportunity as concept, I'm gonna create an actual offer, okay? I'm gonna create something that makes that possible. Okay, so this becomes the stack slide, and we'll talk about stack in the next session, but the stack is like, here's all the things we're gonna get, okay? Now what I want you guys to understand is, the difference between a product versus an offer, okay? This is like one of the most important things that hopefully you'll get from today. Most of us in business, we think of things as a product. Here's the product that I sell, okay? I sell this water bottle, I sell this marker, I sell this phone, right? And the problem with the product is it's a commodity, right? So what happens is like, and this happens a ton in the car industry, or any industry, right? Like, let's say I'm gonna buy this phone, okay? Here's the phone, and I'm gonna Google it, and I'm gonna say, how much do the new iPhone cost? And it's like, if it's on this website, it's $500 from this 300, whatever you want, I'm gonna buy the cheapest one, because it's a commodity, right? I don't care what it is, they're all the same, I just want the cheapest one possible, okay? And so a lot of you guys, that's the business you're playing in. I see it's a lot of people who sell stuff on Amazon, or on Shopify, like physical products are the worst of this. Network marketers are the worst of this, because they're selling the same thing that 10,000 of the people are selling, so everyone's just, it's a race to the bottom, right? So I go to Amazon, type in, I want a new security system, and look at all the things I'm gonna find, the one's got the highest reviews and lowest price, and that's who wins, right? It's all based on that, so it's a race to the bottom when you're a product, okay? Now I hate businesses that are product-based businesses, because I hate being the cheapest guy. One of my mentors, Dan Kennedy, said that if you can't be the lowest price leader in town, there is zero strategic advantage about being the second lowest price leader. So you can't be the lowest price leader, be the most expensive, because there's a huge strategic advantage of being the most expensive. So how do I be the most expensive? How do I take this iPhone, and how do I sell for $10,000? I guarantee you guys, I can sell everyone of you, not all of you, but at least 15 to 20% of you guys, I can sell $10,000 on our iPhone right now if I wanted to. How would I do that? A couple things, first off I would do, is I would figure out this stuff, I'd figure out, okay, because if I try to make this offer to the masses, it's not gonna work. I gotta figure out which is the market that I'm going into. So if I wanna sell this iPhone for $10,000, because everyone else is trying to get the cheapest price possible, I wanna jack up the price, because I wanna be the highest price leader in town, not the cheapest, what am I gonna do? Okay, am I gonna go relationships, health or wealth? I think I'm gonna go wealth. Okay, so now I'm inside wealth. Now this is the bloody water, how, like what's the market I'm gonna go into? I'm gonna go into the internet marketing, because I understand that. And then my blue ocean over here is gonna be, is gonna be, let's say funnels, or let's say it's copyright, or whatever it is, right? So I pick my blue ocean, okay? So there's my blue ocean, now somehow I have to sell this phone for $10,000. How am I gonna do that? Okay, well I need to make a really, really good offer. Okay, right now everyone else has a phone, and maybe they get better battery life, or faster battery life, maybe they're gonna get another plug, a better mouse trap, right? That's an improvement offer. I wanna make this an opportunity to switch. I wanna make this, it's not just a phone you can call people on, this is a phone that actually changes your life. So how do I do that? I gotta create a freaking irresistible offer. So the first thing you're gonna get when you invest my package today, is you are gonna get an iPhone. Okay, the product, that's what most people are selling. Total value of $497, right? Now the next thing you're gonna get, is inside of this phone, what I'm gonna do is I'm gonna go through every single training course I have ever done, from the beginning time till now, teach people how to build funnels. In fact, the first course, people spent $10,000 to come here in this office, I recorded the whole thing, I'm gonna put all those videos on here. So there's a $10,000 funnel course, total value here of $10,000. Then I'm gonna install a special app here, this app's called Voxer, and inside of Voxer there's like a walkie-talkie app. I'm actually gonna plug my secret password and username into that walkie-talkie app, so you can message me questions anytime you want, whenever you want, and ask me questions about how to do this for your specific business. So I'm gonna voxer access to me, total value of, right now we actually literally sell at $50,000, part of my inner circle, so it's a $50,000 thing here. Next thing I'm gonna do is I'm gonna give you a secret, I'm gonna bookmark inside of your browser, inside of Safari, I'm gonna bookmark the top 25 sites that I get all my products from, my services from, my vendors, my everything, everyone I use, I'm gonna bookmark me here, so all my top bookmarks for everyone I use to build my entire multimillion dollar company, value of that is like $19.97. And then the last thing I'm gonna give you, and nobody gets this, I'm gonna give you my phone number. I mean, if you ever start, just call me on the phone, okay? And total value of this right here is $120,000, but I'm gonna give you that phone for just one small payment of $10,000. How do you guys buy that right now? Holy crap, Russell, how did you take this product that everybody can buy anywhere on planet earth for the exact same price, and you turned it into an offer, now you sell the exact same thing for $10,000? So it decomoditized the thing. Does that make sense, you guys? When we decomoditize the thing, price becomes irrelevant, okay? If it's a commodity, there's huge price barriers, because everyone's like, oh, it's cheaper here, faster here, da da da da, right? With ClickFunnels, there's people that do parts of what ClickFunnels is for cheaper, or for, you know, there's little ERs, little better things, right? The difference is I've given people a new opportunity, okay, they got a software, they can't get this anywhere else. I'm the only person on planet earth that can offer this, therefore I can charge whatever I want for it, okay? Price is no longer, I've stripped out the commoditization of my business, and now I can become the highest price leader, okay? Do not do a race to the bottom, okay?
